rulings.Case Title: JOHN M SILVA VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative, A Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 11, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Republic Supply Company's motion for summary judgment, and/or in the alternative, motion for summary adjudication is denied pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 2.7(B) and 2.11(T) for failure to lodge with the Court a courtesy copy of defendant's moving papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SALINAS VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 11, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant's failure to support its motion with any evidence precludes defendant from successfully sustaining its burden. (See CRC 3.1350(c).) The Court will not consider defendant's request in its reply brief to continue the hearing, as defendant already made such request to the Court in an ex parte application, which the Court denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
